title: lilium bulbiferum subsp. jankae
intro: Lilium bulbiferum subsp. jankae, commonly known as the Jankae Lily, is a subspecies of the Lilium bulbiferum plant, which is a type of lily native to the Balkan Peninsula. The bulb of the Jankae Lily has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the bulb of the Jankae Lily is typically dried and used as a powder or tea, or it can be made into a tincture or infused oil for topical application.
